title: Race-blocked CatBoost on raw high-cardinality categoricals
summary: The current best solutions hand-engineer many frequency/interactions for `Driver`, `Race`, and `Compound`, but CatBoost was designed to turn categorical values and combinations into ordered target statistics while reducing boosting bias.
rationale: The current best solutions hand-engineer many frequency/interactions for `Driver`, `Race`, and `Compound`, but CatBoost was designed to turn categorical values and combinations into ordered target statistics while reducing boosting bias. With 800+ drivers and repeated race/compound contexts, it can capture interaction structure the manual encodings likely miss.
implementation_hint: Train `CatBoostClassifier` with raw categorical columns plus the strongest numeric pit-window features, using folds grouped by `Year+Race`. Drop most global frequency encodings to avoid redundancy, keep depth modest, and compare pure CatBoost vs a blend with the current GBDT baseline. Ensure every OOF prediction is generated from race-held-out folds only.
expected_effect: Could improve CV slightly on its own, but the bigger value may be a more diverse error pattern for ensembling than another LightGBM-style feature variant.
risk: If folds are not strictly race-blocked, ordered categorical statistics will look better than they really are. If numeric features dominate and CatBoost is under-tuned, it may only match the baseline.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
